interprets the word as i.e. rural people; but the
author means only those who live by catching and selling fish,
oysters, shells and the like.]
i
If the affected asterism be Asle?a, the chief of the Asikas
would be destroyed; if it be Magha, the ruler of Anga would meet
with his end; if it be Purvaphalguni, the Pandya king would quit
the world; if it be Uttaraphalgun!, the governor of Ujjain would
be afflicted; and if it be Hasta, the lord of the Danqlaka forest
would be destroyed.
[The state of Ahga is very ancient being mentioned in the
Atharvaveda (V-22.14), the Aitareya Brahmana (VIII-22). During the
time of the Buddha its capital was Gampa. This country is men-
tioned in the Ramayana also (1.23). Regarding Asikade^a many
theories are held : Some read Masikanagara in its stead. So some
locate it to the west of Kalinga. Others take it as Rsika. Even
this is not accepted by scholars owing to geographical anomalies.]
